title: Efficient and accurate neural-field reconstruction using resistive memory
methods: implicit neural field representation (MLP + Gaussian positional encoding)
data_sources: NeRF synthetic dataset (Mildenhall et al., Commun. ACM 2021, doi:10.1145/3503250)

data_scope.ct_benchmark: Pancreas 4D CT — publicly available; exact scan count not stated in abstract
data_scope.nerf_benchmark: NeRF synthetic dataset — 8 Blender synthetic scenes (standard benchmark)
data_scope.hardware_platform: 40-nm CMOS resistive-memory macro, 256 Kb; 180-nm CMOS analogue readout

core_conclusion: A software-hardware co-optimization framework pairs implicit neural-field representations (compressed via low-rank decomposition and structured pruning) with a resistive-memory computing-in-memory chip to reconstruct complex signals from sparse inputs, achieving up to 32.3x energy-efficiency and 38.8x parallelism gains over digital baselines across 3D CT reconstruction, novel view synthesis, and dynamic-scene synthesis — without sacrificing reconstruction quality.
why_it_matters: Breaks the von Neumann bottleneck for AI inference at the edge; demonstrates simultaneous energy and parallelism gains across three distinct reconstruction tasks; enables sparse-input AI reconstruction under the tight power and bandwidth budgets of medical devices, AR/VR, and embodied robots; provides a reproducible open-source co-design methodology.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary result spanning materials science, hardware design, and machine learning; validated on a fabricated 40-nm chip (not simulation only); directly addresses societal impact of medical imaging AI in resource-limited settings; pioneers the neural-field-on-resistive-memory paradigm as a conceptual step-change in AI hardware co-design.
added_value_to_my_research: Neural-field compression applied to spatiotemporal geospatial fields (population density, mobility flow tensors) from sparse sensor observations — a hardware-accelerated alternative to KDE
Hardware-aware co-design loop adapted to prune/quantize geospatial transformers for on-device spatial inference (e.g., real-time 2SFCA scoring on embedded health monitors)
Stochastic Gaussian encoder variance as a proxy for observation-density-aware uncertainty quantification in spatial prediction models (MGWR, ensemble ML)
Sparse-input neural field applied to multi-source remote-sensing data fusion — gap-filling incomplete satellite imagery as a continuous spatiotemporal surface
Coupling efficient CT reconstruction with a spatial accessibility layer to produce a composite 'effective healthcare access' metric that accounts for both travel time and on-site AI compute capacity

related.title: Enhancing deep learning-based field reconstruction with a differentiable learning framework
related.relation: field reconstruction from sparse sensors — complementary software-side approach to the paper's hardware-accelerated neural-field method

related.title: Deep Bayesian active learning using in-memory computing hardware
related.relation: in-memory computing hardware for AI inference — shared hardware paradigm (CIM) applied to active learning rather than signal reconstruction

related.title: Resistive memory-based zero-shot liquid state machine for multimodal event data learning
related.relation: resistive memory as computational substrate — parallel work using resistive memory for neuromorphic/multimodal learning

related.title: Energy-efficient multimodal zero-shot learning using in-memory reservoir computing
related.relation: energy-efficient in-memory computing for multimodal learning — shared goal of neuromorphic energy efficiency

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: in-memory computing with spiking neural networks — earlier work establishing the CIM paradigm for deep learning

figures.caption: Cross-level co-optimizations — overview of the software (neural field + compression) and hardware (resistive-memory CIM) co-design layers
HAQ for accurate in-memory matrix multiplications — weight mapping, convergence analysis, and programming cycles
Architecture and circuits of the hardware co-design — fabricated 40-nm resistive-memory macro and 180-nm analogue readout
3D CT sparse reconstruction — qualitative and quantitative comparison; 23.5x energy and 10.8x parallelism gains
Novel view synthesis — NeRF synthetic benchmark results; 21.0x energy and 38.8x parallelism gains
Dynamic-scene novel view synthesis — D-NeRF benchmark; 32.3x energy and 6.2x parallelism gains
